summary: Postinstall credential stealer exfiltrating SSH keys,.env files, and secret env vars to hardcoded IP
details: @wagni_bot/eth-agent@1.1.1 executes postinstall.js automatically on npm install. The script scans the current working directory, the user's home directory, and ~/.ssh for files matching.pem,.key, id_rsa, id_ed25519,.cred, and.env, reads their contents, and additionally filters process.env for keys containing PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, and AWS. It tags the collected data with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and process.cwd(), then POSTs the bundle over plain HTTP to the hardcoded endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package presents itself as an 'Unofficial eth-agent SDK' but contains no legitimate SDK content — the only install-time effect is the credential harvester, consistent with a namespace-abuse lure targeting developers searching for an Ethereum agent SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
